# Anti-Patterns — What Makes Ideas Unsticky

**Anti-Pattern 1: The Curse of Knowledge. Assuming your audience knows what you know.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Burying the Lead. The most important point buried in details.**

**Case: The Jargon. Experts use jargon. It kills understanding.**

**Case: The Abstract. "Synergy" is abstract. "Two people pulling a rope" is concrete.**

**Case: The Statistics Dump. A list of numbers is forgettable. A vivid example is not.**

**Case: The No-Story. A message without a story has no emotional hooks.**
---

**Case: The Mission Statement. Most corporate mission statements are abstract and meaningless.**
**Case: The Death by PowerPoint. Slides full of text bullet points kill ideas.**
**Case: The Abstract Strategy. A strategy that cannot be explained simply will not be executed.**
**Case: The Data Overload. More data does not mean more persuasion. It means more confusion.**
**Case: The Boiling Frog. The metaphor is vivid. But it is factually wrong. Unsticky ideas are based on bad data.**
**Case: The Generic. Generic messages blend in. Specific messages stand out.**
**Case: The Curse. The curse of knowledge is the hardest problem. It requires constant vigilance.**
**Case: The Default. The default communication style is abstract, formal, and boring. That is the enemy.**
